question:
Which sentence tells an opinion?

A) Dogs have four legs.
B) Dogs are the best pets.
hints: An opinion tells what someone thinks or feels. A fact is something that is always true.
Which sentence tells what someone believes, not something you can prove?
solution: B is an opinion because 'best' tells what someone thinks. Not everyone agrees that dogs are the best pets. A is a fact because all dogs do have four legs.

question:
Which word helps start an opinion?

A) I think
B) There are
C) It has
hints: An opinion tells what someone believes or feels.
Which words show that someone is sharing what they believe?
solution: 'I think' is the best way to start an opinion because it tells the reader you are sharing your own belief. 'There are' and 'It has' are used for facts or descriptions.

question:
This opinion is missing a reason. Pick the best reason to add.

Opinion: I think recess is the best part of school.

A) Recess is 20 minutes long.
B) We play outside during recess.
C) It is fun because we get to run and play with friends.
hints: A good reason tells WHY you have your opinion.
Which choice explains why recess is the BEST part of school?
solution: C is the best reason because it explains WHY recess is the best — it is fun, and you get to run and play with friends. A just states a fact about recess. B tells what happens but does not explain why it is the best.

question:
Read this opinion writing. What is missing?

"I think pizza is the best food. It has yummy cheese on top."

A) An opinion
B) A reason
C) A closing sentence
hints: Good opinion writing has three parts: opinion, reason, and a closing.
The opinion is 'pizza is the best food.' The reason is 'yummy cheese on top.' What is missing?
solution: The writing is missing a closing sentence. It has an opinion ('pizza is the best food') and a reason ('yummy cheese on top') but needs an ending like 'That is why pizza is my favorite!'

question:
Put these sentences in the correct order for opinion writing. Type the letter order (example: BCA).

A) That is why summer is my favorite season.
B) I think summer is the best season.
C) We get to swim and play outside every day.
hints: Opinion writing starts with your opinion, then gives a reason, then has a closing.
Which sentence states the opinion? That goes first.
Which sentence gives the reason? That goes in the middle. The closing sentence goes last.
solution: The correct order is B-C-A. B states the opinion ('summer is the best season'), C gives the reason ('swim and play outside'), and A is the closing ('That is why summer is my favorite').
